events.note: RDP logon — Logon_Type=10 is the definitive RDP indicator
Special privileges assigned at logon — attacker account has admin rights
mstsc.exe network connection to RDP port — Sysmon catches the initiating process

mutation_hints.objective: Establish interactive RDP session to target host for hands-on-keyboard access
detection_anchors: Field match: Logon_Type=10 (RemoteInteractive) IS the RDP anchor — cannot be changed without losing the technique
Field match: DestinationPort=3389 → change to 3388 or 13389 (some orgs move RDP to alternate ports); also port 3391 used by some RDP gateway configs
Field match: Image=mstsc.exe → use C:\Windows\SysWOW64\mstsc.exe (32-bit client) or tscon.exe (session reconnect/hijack variant that doesn't open a new logon)
Field match: Logon_Process=User32 → change to NtLmSsp if NTLM is forced, or Kerberos for domain auth path
Field match: Authentication_Package=Negotiate → some rules key on this; NTLM or Kerberos are also valid values depending on the domain policy
Field: Source_Network_Address → use a different internal pivot IP that is in the known-good range for the target
Field: DestinationHostname → FQDN vs NetBIOS name vs empty (some rules match on hostname format)
